text.content:
Total mission setup time: 1+ hours. Much of that should be spent figuring out how you want to run the Vampyres.

The Afterlife's grand opening marches closer, and the Frenchman wants to make sure it opens in style. Fortunately, he has access to one of the most constant promotional tools in human history - a team of mercs with a zeal for a bigger reputation (that's you guys!).

This mission unlocks the @UUID[JournalEntry.SDvTzlKjvOpgC3EC]{7th Circle's services on completion}, success or fail. While players are in the bar at staff request after that point, they do not need to pay to get in, though they don't receive any bonuses without paying.

Important Note: This mission makes use of the Philharmonic Vampyre mooks, which are one of the more complex gangs in the pack. Instructions for playing them have been included at the end of this journal, but you should take care to familiarize yourself with how they work (and possibly choose their set-dressing for the beat) before beginning the session.

Robbin The Hood

Friend Opportunity: Sammy Chauvin

Sammy is a social butterfly - a people person, somebody who knows everyone. If you have him as a friend, if you already knew the name of the person you're looking for, you can use his Lowdown skill for checks involving their whereabouts or personality, in place of any skills of your own. Assuming you can call or meet up and ask, of course.

Enemy Opportunity: NCPD Captain Or Vampyre MC

An NCPD captain in Little Europe is a known bastard, and even deeper in the pocket of the local corps than the rest of the force. Alternately, a prominent Vampyre MC jealously guards their little slice of the limelight. One or the other can be replaced by a player enemy, if a player has a relevant one.

Rep Opportunity: Standing Ovation

Track the stunts being pulled during the heist - the character which pulls the most difficult and elaborate trick (measured by DV) wins the achievement.

Gig Payout

4 Eagle base payout/player, plus whatever they nab from the vault, and a 2 eagle bonus for sticking to Sammy's "script" in terms of lethality (or lack thereof).

Beat Chart

Beat 1: Meeting Sammy & Vido

The 7th Circle, the Afterlife's bar, casino and meeting space, is almost ready to open - all that's left is to promote it. Vido introduces players to the bartender, who gives them a rather unusual promo job.

Beat 2: Gearing Up

Before the players can rob their target, they'll need a special bit of kit to do so - a shard for the bank, and rubber bullets for the Vamps.

Beat 3: Upstaging The Vampyres

The players "meet" the Vampyres mid job, and attempt to upstage their performance.

Beat 4: The First National Bank of Charlie-Foxtrot

The NCPD intervenes, and they're not impressed by the performance.

Beat 5: Counting The Loot

You got paid, the Vamps got their show, the Afterlife gets its grand opening - everyone wins! Except the people you robbed, I guess. And maybe the NCPD.

the FM Receiver Crackles, The Pirate Wavelength Carrying a Cool, Seductive Female Voice…

"Good evening my little Nightcrawlers. This is Nightlight, the voice of the city, taking the scream off of the sheets and into your ears with a quiet murmur for those of you too busy to read. Whether you're on a car, bike, board or heels, from my mouth to your ears: keep those eyes on the street. Lets talk news.

"Our top story tonight - little miss Nightlight got a hot tip from an anonymous, trusted source, that the scamming and sharking office otherwise known as the Central Bank of Night City is about to be sucked dry and spat out. A little birdie says to keep your eyes open tomorrow afternoon, and to make sure you come in and make those loan payments if you have 'em - because this time, the bank might be paying you. Just watch out - the first three rows are the splash zone.

"We also got a hot tip on a new bounty - a beautiful pristine sixty Gs for anyone who brings the head of Silvers the Solo to a certain Petrochem exec who asked not to be named. Evidently the cold-blooded queen killed his kid and absconded with her arsenal, sometime in the last couple of days, and that certain someone's looking for revenge. So if you feel like getting your hands dirty and trying your luck against the most dangerous merc in the city, well, there's a year's living in it for you. Let's just see how high that bounty goes, hm?

"Otherwise, I've got a thirst-quenching thank you from a certain someone surrounding that bounty on rare drinks. Mister Chauvin says his cabinets and kegs are locked, stocked and loaded, and his rendition of a certain heaven on Earth will be opening soon, somewhere in the Upper Marina. You heard it here first my little Nightingales, and you can be sure little miss Nightlight will be first on the scene, as soon as they're serving.

"That's it for this evening's news. Coming up, we've got a soundtrack for victory - anthems from decades past and present, marching into the future. This is Nightlife, signing off, and hoping she's made your night a little brighter.

In this beat, the party learns how Vido is planning to promote the Afterlife's bar before it opens in a few days.

This mission makes use of the @UUID[Scene.fqgvewCqt8z94mll]{7th Circle Bar & Casino} and @UUID[Scene.vboI8hWM5vr7ZfIB]{Night City Bank} scenes, along with a few environments. To begin:

  • Place a token for @UUID[Actor.17CTAE5lKmZFHY9i]{Vido Dubois} in the bar scene, on one of the stools at the bar. He's waiting to meet the players.

  • Place a token for @UUID[Actor.rQdrdTOYV13yilZD]{Sammy Chauvin} in the scene, behind the counter at the bar. He's the Afterlife's prospective bartender.

  • Place a token for @UUID[Actor.PJBDw6Qy61QZGIXp]{Silvers} inside the bar, next to the door. She's acting as bouncer and is there to let the players in, leaving afterwards.

  • Place the player tokens outside the door, in the hallway beyond.

  • Change the scene to @UUID[Scene.VCbthP8Ez4uhtdkr]{Afterlife Exterior Environment.}

Then read the following:

Afterlife Interior - Basement Level 1

The evening is late and the Afterlife glowing red over the streets of the Upper Marina. The city is unusually quiet tonight - or maybe you're just too used to the overpacked suburbs and combat zones, where gunshots have become a natural part of your background ambience.

Each of you received a text from Vido, only a few hours ago:

"Bonjour, mis vachers!"

(French slang for 'cowherd,' and also 'edgerunner')

"I hope you enjoy staying up late, because the Afterlife's grand opening is only a few days away, and we have need of you for our promotion! Assuming you are available, myself and the Afterlife's new bartender will be waiting for you in the first basement level at 8:00 this evening. As per last time, I am told that the drinks will be complementary."

If players knock at the front door to the Afterlife, they can easily coerce Sprinter into letting them into the area without paying, though they won't get the rep bonus for doing so. Sprinter notes that this will be "the last time" since "the club's openin' up day after tomorrow."

Once the players are inside, Sprinter greets them cheerfully, asks after their health and well being, then ushers them downstairs via the hallway ramp in the back left corner of the lobby. She's excited about the opening, having been involved in the project for much longer than you all.

Once players have cleared the lobby, read the following.

The glowing lobby is left behind as you descend, for the first time, into the deeper reaches of the Afterlife. The ramp downwards is well lit, and Sprinter chats amicably about this-and-that in her personal life as she walks you down.

The first stop on the right is a steel-plated door that looks like it could take a shot from a GunMart Engage and stay standing. Like at the entrance, there's a coin slot where the door handle should be, and a metal shutter hiding a viewpoint - or muzzlepoint - at eye level.

Rather than depositing a coin, Sprinter raps her knuckles on the metal door twice. "'Ello!" she calls. "Sammy? You in there?"

The shutter slides open for a moment, and you catch the glint of steely grey eyes, before it slides shut again without a word. The door clicks as a heavy bolt is moved, and it opens.

"Have fun, loves!" Sprinter says as she turns to head back up the ramp. "And go easy on the new guy, yeah? He seems nice."

(Switch the scene to The 7th Circle)

Inside the 7th Circle

Beyond the door is Silvers, evidently acting as bouncer to the club beyond. Inside is an extravagant sight that's an even match for the lobby, albeit in an entirely different way.

The central floor flickers and glows in a pixelated LED simulation of fire. Directly in front of you on the right is a dance floor in front of a stage, pulsing with light and illumination at a much more intense rate.

On your left, the rest of the club stretches out. A casino sits pristine and as-yet unused in a pit on the east side of the room - above it dangle decorative chains and cages. On the room's west wall is a well-stocked, well-illuminated bar fronted by a row of stools. Beyond it is a seating area - each table has an expensive looking holographic projector installed in its center.

At the back of the room are four booths, each a different color from the last - purple, green, blue, orange - and on the left and right sides of the row are doors, swung open to reveal a more private meeting space, if one was needed.

It's quiet and empty save for some hard rock playing quietly near the bar, but you suspect that it won't remain that way for much longer.

Vido and Sammy are sitting at the bar, but Silvers exits the club once you're all inside. Evidently, you can be trusted not to trash the place, for the moment. Vido waves in a friendly fashion, and the man behind the bar smiles at you. He's stupendously handsome in a way Vido could never be, with toned muscle visible on his arms and beneath his tight shirt. He's wearing a banana-yellow vest, and his hair has been carefully groomed, as has his flamboyant and well-waxed mustache and goatee.

Now would be a good time to show your party the artwork for Sammy.

"Howdy, fellas (and ladyfellas)," he says, speaking in a light and pleasant drawl. "Sammy Chauvin, at your service. Pull up a stool, why don't ya? I've got work, and Vido says you're just the man for it."

Roleplaying Exercise - In Loving Memory

As players sit down, Vido informs Sammy that the drinks are on him, for theariety of booze and other ingredients behind the counter. Each player needs to specify day, and each party member can choose to create a drink of their choice from the wide v at least 2 ingredients, and in true Afterlife tradition, if they flatline in an adequately spectacular fashion, their personal taste (or lack thereof) will be available for purchase in their memory henceforth.

Some memorable examples from my own games:

  • Neon Gas: Lambda, one of the edgerunners included in NCG&C, drinks Neon Gas - one part grain alcohol that's one step away from being refined into CHOOH2, four parts energy drink, with a cheap neon additive stirred in that glows going down (and coming out). Often mixed with drugs, since it already tastes foul. But it sure makes you go-go-go!

  • Red Juju: Cyber Warlord, another edgerunner, drinks Red Juju - a short glass of iced whiskey, mixed with a healthy portion of cinnamon and a shot of hot sauce.

  • Sacred Herb Smash: xXDreadedElfKillerXx, an Elflines cosplayer/fixer, drinks a mix of proprietary "sacred herbs" from an Elflines-sponsored food line, with a can of smash mixed in.

  • Kuchikamizake: named after the first alcoholic beverage in Japan. 1 part sake, 1 part any soju (korean plum wine), 1 part yuzu juice (a citrus fruit). It's drunk by Sirius, an Arasaka-aligned exec.

  • Desert Rose: Six, a cowboy-themed solo, drinks a Desert Rose - 2 parts tequila, 2 parts cactus juice, 1/2 oz Lime Juice, a pinch of salt, iced, with a lime wheel for garnish.

  • Poison Ivy: Ivy, a shy-but-eccentric medtech, drinks Poison Ivy - a base of Midori (bright green Japanese melon liquor) with an absolute ton of lemon/lime additives to make it as sour as possible.

Those who participate may earn 1 background point if that system is being utilized, which expires at the end of the mission - otherwise, they simply gain +3 to their next check.

Once everyone's settled, Sammy begins talking about the job.

Talking As Sammy

In case you didn't get the idea yet, Sammy is ridiculously hot, coolly confident, and easy to get along with. In short, it's easy to get lost while talking with him.

Any player with a COOL stat of less than 6 must make a DV13 COOL Concentration check.

On failure:

  • If the character is sexually attracted to men, they're more than a little abashed, and might stumble over words.

  • If they're not, they suddenly feel rather insecure about themselves by comparison, and should roleplay accordingly.

Sammy talks in a drawl that'd be typical of the California countryside some 40 years ago. He's easygoing and laughs at his problems - see his mook sheet for more details.

Job Details

  • The Afterlife is opening its doors to the public about 48 hours after the conversation takes place - or at least some parts are.

    • Sammy notes that the club and lobby are ready to go, but the gambling den and hotel rooms will be future jobs, after this one is finished.

  • He's brought the players in to promote the club in a rather unusual way.

    • He has an old friend/rival in the Philharmonic Vampyres, and they're robbing a major bank in Little Europe.

    • The players will be upstaging them to make headlines, something that the Vampyres excel at.

      • This won't be the only promotion, but the club is for edgerunners, so the club's appealing to the community by showing a gig worthy of the 2020s.

  • Don't worry about any legal consequences for the robbery, once they're off the scene.

    • Between the Afterlife and the Vampyres, and the known fact that it's a bank which the mob - AKA Night Corp - uses to launder money, it's a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to shoot at the NCPD without meaningful repercussions… mostly.

  • Sammy notes that this gig needs to be non lethal.

    • The Vampyres have a reputation as vigilantes - the kind of people that are after Sammy's own heart, and part of that reputation is their use of rubber bullets and, generally speaking, leaving whoever they're messing with alive to tell the tale.

    • Doubling down on that, there's no way the NCPD won't respond, and no amount of "alternative facts" in the press or grassroots activism from Vampyre fans will get the cops to drop the case if any of them end up dead on the job.

      • That's not to say the cops won't be using live ammo, of course.

  • He also notes that, thanks to an anonymous tip to Nightlight, the bank and its occupants know the heist is going down.

    • The players will face ready opposition, and an audience will be present.

    • Don't worry about the police waiting in the wings, though.

      • The NCPD are too understaffed to keep anyone on site just for a mere maybe, especially when making such a threat to ruffle feathers and then not showing up would set an unfortunate precedent for being an excellent prank.

      • That's also why the bank's still open - stubborn managers not willing to lose a day of profits on a maybe.

  • On the gig, players will be competing for attention, pulling stunts and making a scene and generally upstaging the Vamps at their own game.

    • Mechanically, this will be accomplished by players asking to perform, being told the roll, and setting the DV for that roll themselves. The higher the DV they pass, the more the payout.

  • Sammy has some assistance waiting in the wings to help players prep for the show - but they should formulate some ideas of their own, as well.

    • At this, he produces a thick briefcase from underneath the bar - "barter materials for your supplier," he says.

      • The address he provides is in Upper Marina, only a few blocks away and in comfortable walking distance.

      • He refuses to elaborate on what the supplier is offering, however.

    • The briefcase is locked via combination lock, but can be opened with a DV15 Pick Lock check. Inside is a week's supply of very high quality fresh food - mostly fruits and veggies.

      • "Don't you worry about the code," Sammy says, "the supplier already knows it."

Once players have exhausted any questions for Sammy, proceed to Beat 2.

In this beat, the party meets a head honcho of the Vampyres, who offers to help them in preparing for their upcoming job - competition makes for the greatest show, after all!

Change the environment to @UUID[Scene.RND2jHUhhcOv5uJH]{Upper Marina Environment,} then read the following:

A Nondescript Storefront In Upper Marina

Like any properly gentrified neighborhood, Upper Marina is a medium commercial/residential district filled to the brim with perfectly acceptable buildings that are utterly unused thanks to starscraping rent prices. It's one of these locations that Sammy has provided you, just a few short blocks from the Afterlife on the district's west side, and one extra layer of brownstone away from turning into the ordered brick streets of Little Europe.

The storefront is locked, its tinted windows blacking out the sun from the outside. A large sign pinned in the window is promoting the future location of Dracula's Harvest, which appears to be some type of theater studio, or maybe a location for studying improv. No further information is provided.

There are two ways into the building - the front and the back.

  • If entering through the front, the door can be opened with a DV17 Pick Lock check (it is Upper Marina, after all).

    • Players can also simply choose to knock.

    • Optionally, have the player roll a luck save to see if an NCPD patrol car drives by while they're attempting the pick.

  • Otherwise, the back door is locked by the same check, but is much more secluded in an alleyway rimming the strip of businesses that the storefront is a part of.

    • An unlocked window adjoins a fire escape above the door, which leads down a flight of stairs to the back exit.

Once players are inside, read the following.

As soon as the last of you enters, the door swings shut and the room goes black.

"AVAST, YE SALTY DAWGS!" Cries a voice. A moment later a spotlight blares bright, revealing that a rickety-looking wooden pirate ship as the room's sole feature. Atop it, a man in an eyepatch and pirate garb thrusts a saber towards you. His teeth glint gold in the sudden light. "Stand to, and relinquish ye treasure, lest ye… ah… hm. Damn it! Line! This is why we need to open the place…"

Speaking As Patrick The Pirate

Patrick is a member of the Vampyres, and on good terms with Sammy Chauvin. He's also the spearhead behind the informal HQ that the gang will be setting up with the bank's proceeds, which the party is currently standing in. He's "new to the pirate role" and will try to speak accordingly. But probably won't do well. Any facedowns will involve him attempting to fight the person making the facedown with his (dulled saber).

  • Patrick is amicable to the party once he learns who they are. He provides the following supplies:

    • Any player may requisition up to 6 magazines each of rubber ammo of any type.

      • He also offers to blunt any relevant melee weapons, upon request - doing so stops the weapons from drawing blood or causing critical injuries and any targets reduced to 0 HP by the weapon are unconscious, but stable.

    • For every 3 members of the party (rounded up), Patrick supplies 1 teargas grenade and 1 smoke grenade.

  • Patrick is excited about the upcoming heist, and feels that any good performance troupe shines brightest when competing for attention, hence his assistance.

    • His troupe is only just getting started, and he wants to help them make a name. Seeing the favor he owed Sammy, he views the situation as a win-win for both sides.

  • Above table, now is also a good time to explain how the upcoming encounter works.

    • Each initiative round, the Vampyres and the party will compete for attention during the robbery. On every player's turn, they may attempt an action of any kind to do so.

      • When doing so, the GM informs the player of the DV for the check.

      • If the player succeeds the DV, they gain the spotlight until someone else in the scene succeeds a check with a DV higher than the one the player set.

      • If the player fails the check, the next check a Vampyre makes is made at a cumulative +3 bonus.

      • At the end of the round, whichever side of the performance holds the spotlight scores a point. Whoever has the most points once Beat 4 begins wins the contest.

    • (Optional): Enhanced assistance:

      • Any actor can choose to hold their turn until a certain point in initiative, allowing for assistance towards a check.

      • Any actor can use their action to assist in a check on their turn in a fashion which seems appropriate.

        • If they exceed the result rolled by the actor they're assisting, the final score of the assisted check is increased by 3.

        • If they tie the result, the final score is increased by 2.

        • If below the result, the final score is increased by 1.

  • Because of this, he also reveals an array of "combat costumes" from crates that are off to the side of the room, and insists that the party choose a theme for their robbery.

    • He'll work with the party to brainstorm a theme for the robbery with which they can compete with the Vampyres.

      • Patrick is partial to the pirate theme, and encourages the party to embrace it, but also suggests an old west robbery, an alien invasion, a Robin Hood theme, Sportsball, or a creative reinterpretation of your personal Tolkein race of choice to suit the occasion.

    • Once costumes are decided on, each party member may add 1 outfit comprised of a mix of Leisurewear/Asia Pop/Urban Flash to their inventory at their discretion, renamed to describe the costume they've chosen.

    • The relevant pieces of this costume have been reinforced with light armorjack.

  • Once costumes are assembled, have each player roll a Wardrobe & Style check while describing their costume.

    • On passing a DV17, players can add 1 to the DV reference for stealing the spotlight, directly after they steal it (it's a little harder for the Vampyres to steal back). This bonus is increased to 2 if they roll 22+.

  • It only occurs to Patrick after everyone's in costume that he's supposed to be paid for his help.

    • Opening the briefcase reveals a week's supply of very high quality fresh food - mostly fruits and veggies. Fruit prevents scurvy!

Once players are outfitted and ready to go, advance time to around midday of the next if you're not already there, and advance to beat 3.

In this beat, the party will attempt to upstage the Vampyres at Night City Bank, robbing the place in the process.

This beat uses the @UUID[Scene.KvOyTclweloOmLEO]{Little Europe Environment} and @UUID[Scene.vboI8hWM5vr7ZfIB]{Night City Bank} scenes. To begin:

  • Place 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.70S20tDJetnmcAa9]{Vampyre MC}, 1 + 1/2 Playercount (rounded up) @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.yzai3h4XK0T0Bzzg]{Vampyre Performers}, and 1/3 Playercount (rounded up) @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.BF54nXCe0HAYzSBS]{Vampyre Showstoppers} in the scene.

  • Place the player tokens on the street outside the bank.

  • Place 2 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.AIR0MIWchnbMvM5D]{RatSec Grunts} at the entrance, keeping watch on the crowds.

  • Place 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.91JT7Zncn3Sb60Ge]{RatSec Elite} and 1/2 partycount @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.AIR0MIWchnbMvM5D]{RatSec Grunts} in the southernmost portion of the admin area of the bank (where the couch is), keeping an eye on the security cameras.

  • Place 1/2 partycount @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.nUF7oF2uBUTM4Ur4]{RatSec Execs} around the scene at your discretion.

  • For every other actor in the scene, place up to 2 civilians, representing bank staff and customers.

  • Set up the Vampyre abilities - distribute luck points for the performers, roll for the Vampyre set dressing, roll a second set dressing for the Showstopper(s), etc.

  • Review the "playing as the Vampyres" journal entry at the end of this mission's journal.

  • Change the scene to the @UUID[Scene.KvOyTclweloOmLEO]{Little Europe Environment.}

Then read the following:

The Bank Of Night City

Nestled comfortably among the brownstone sprawl of Little Europe, the marbled halls of Night City Bank have survived every calamity to befall the city since the collapse, robbing its citizens with interest and collections all the while. The bank is mostly a front for Night Corp and its various factions to store and transfer money through, laundering it for the mafia that runs the corp or else financing the various discrete power struggles among its members. Even still, by day it maintains a flourishing business front by financing loans for desperate Night Citizens who don't want to go to the gangs, and maintaining/strongarming the exorbitant investment portfolios the debt finances across the cityscape.

The bank's gilded doors are bustling even more heavily than usual. A pair of security guards are accompanied by cameras and an autoturret to watch the crowd, but it's readily apparent that they're woefully insufficient to fully track the ins and outs of the bank's customers. Where the Vampyres are - and just how many of these people are here exclusively to see if they make good on their promise - remains to be seen.

Once Inside…

Well-polished stone floors reflect light around the room in a dazzling display. To the north are bathrooms and a manager's office, while more offices obstruct the center of the room. On their other side, a terminal has been set up to allow for access to an underground series of lockboxes which can be rented by bank members. Further along, a row of teller's booths are serving customers; through the window which watches the floor, a staffed security station watches the area with suspicion.

Robbing The Place

While players compete with the Vampyres for attention, both parties are also obviously trying to rob the place. They can do so as follows:

  • The petty cash vault (adjacent to the main lobby) holds 5 actions worth of valuables. Its doorway is secured by a DV11 Netrunning check or a DV17 Electronics/Security Tech or Pick Lock check. The interior gate is opened by taking an additional action (no DV) once the outer doors have been opened.

  • The main vault (back of the building) holds 10 actions worth of valuables. Its doorway is secured by a DV14 netrunning check or a DV21 Electronics/Security Tech or Pick Lock check. The interior gate requires a DV15 Electronics/Security Tech or Pick Lock check, or a DV9 Netrunning check.

While in a vault, any actor can grab a loot box as an action, stowing it on their person. Loot boxes are sealed until opened as an additional action, at which point the player takes the next sequential entry from the results table - see Beat 5 for more details.

Choreographing the Vampyres

Check out my notes (Testing Session - How I Planned My Vampyres) for how I did it; you're welcome to use them as an instruction manual if you don't want to build your own directions.

Dealing With RatSec

The RatSec forces will prioritize guarding the vaults, up to and including if the execs' lives are threatened. They will freely shoot at anyone who they can reasonably hit, and might even fire at civilians in the process. If they use suppressive fire, the effect can be cancelled by an un-suppressed Vampyre successfully attacking the perpetrator and offering assurances to the crowd.

Proceeding To The Next Scene

The NCPD is called as soon as the Vampyres appear (remember, they were expecting this to happen) but don't arrive until one or more of the following occurs:

  • Either faction's tally of spotlight points reaches 4.

  • Half of either faction's members are rendered unconscious or killed

  • 2 or more civilians are killed, or 4 or more are injured

  • 4 or more looting actions are taken, total, between the two vaults.

pages.name: The First National Bank of Charlie-Foxtrot (Beat 4)

text.content:
In this beat, the NCPD arrives to try and deal with the bank robbery and incapacitate the robbers.

Flashing Lights And Sirens Are Heard Outside…

After the contest for the headline has been concluded, an NCPD patrol response, tier 3, arrives to set up a perimeter and attempts to negotiate a surrender, placing one patrol car at the right-rear entrance outside the large bank vault, and the other two at the front of the bank. Off screen, an additional tier 3 response covers other exits (such as to the west of the alleyway which runs the back of the bank).

  • If resistance is encountered, or at GM discretion up your response to tier 4.

  • Once the perimeter is established, the NCPD captain in charge of the perimeter will demand those inside surrender via megaphone.

    • If no response is given but the party has not made it known they're taking hostages, the tier-3 response will breach after 5 rounds.

      • Note: this does not include the aforementioned perimeter control which is off the map.

  • If a response is given and/or it's clear that hostages have been taken, a SWAT response is issued instead, arriving in an additional 1 minute and breaching at GM discretion.

Do What You Want 'Cause A Pirate Is Free!

As soon as the police sirens sound, the Vampyres will give up attempting to hinder the party in any way and immediately move to grab what they can before leaving out the back alley; like the rest of their performance, this was planned from the start.

If the party has engaged the Vampyres with lethal force, the Vamps will continue to fight back, but prioritize getting their wounded members to safety rather than stay to fight a losing battle.

Once they've distributed/stolen as much of the bank's property as they can, they head to the back exit for an escape. To the west, those eligible hear music and shortly thereafter, the patrol car holding the off-map perimeter to the alley's west is demolished. In its place is a generic truck with a combat plow, which has been modified into something akin to a pirate ship parade float by Patrick the Pirate, dropping anchor just long enough to pick up whatever Vampyres can make it to him, and to wish the party a fond farewell.

You Are A Pirate!

(backup link in case the first dies again)

Can I Get An Autograph?

At GM discretion, based on your desired game tone…

In a hilarious plot twist that no one could've envisioned, the NCPD might well be stoked to bust a traditional bank robbery instead of fighting living nightmare booster gangsters, and that excitement and enthusiasm comes through - they'll be amicable to those who surrender and will look the other way while they escape so long as no one is outwardly, lethally violent (arrested players lose any loot, though).

NCPD Response Tiers (for reference)

Tier 3 - Perimeter Control: three @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.WpPo3phuzMNPfYoY]{NCPD Patrol Cars} are dispatched, each containing three @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.jDl9ukUDoVPjplJc]{Patrol Officers} and a @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.yK8PnIHBoxz5wfqg]{SWAT Officer.} These officers will create a perimeter and assess the situation while attempting to prevent anyone from leaving the scene, but will not directly engage agitators unless they attempt to breach the perimeter, instead calling in a Suppression team to take down dangerous players while notifying MaxTac to remain on standby.

Tier 4 - Suppression Dispatch: an @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.Ksf7hMC2dK8WAxs0]{NCPD Armored Transport} is dispatched, containing an eight-person team of @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.yK8PnIHBoxz5wfqg]{SWAT Officers.}

Triumphant Returns

After the robbery, players might wish to return to the Afterlife.

  • If they do, Vido will greet them gladly regardless of the result, so long as a robbery was attempted.

  • Sammy's opinion of the party will quickly sour if they killed any civilians or Vampyres, though a little bit of spilled blood is overlooked.

  • Both will be intensely amused but otherwise unbothered if the players end up arrested, but an arrest won't otherwise affect their standing with either.

When the players return after attempting the robbery, the Afterlife opens its doors the following day. All players gain 1 personal rep for being so instrumental in its success. If they managed to upstage the Vampyres and steal the headline, they gain 2 rep instead.

Their actions have also unlocked the 7th Circle Bar, Club & Bounty Board. @UUID[JournalEntry.SDvTzlKjvOpgC3EC]{See the associated journal entry for more.} If you haven't already, you should also make it visible to your players - you can do so by right clicking it, selecting "Configure Ownership", and changing the "All Players" dropdown to "Observer."

What's In The Box??!

Players open stolen boxes without replacement; if more than 10 boxes were looted, each additional box is worth 150eb.

These rewards can be increased at GM discretion, if players thought ahead to try and ensure there would be, for example, more cash than normal in the vaults during the robbery.

  • Result 1 - 1000eb

  • Result 2 - 500eb

  • Result 3 - 250eb

  • Result 4 - a piece of art, sold via a trading check; the base price (result of 16 or below) is 500eb; worth full value of 750eb at 17+; worth 1000eb at 21+.

  • Result 5 - A bottle of extremely high end liquor; can be sold to Sammy Chauvin for 5 Eagles.

  • Result 6 - Materials to turn 2 regular quality melee weapons into excellent quality melee weapons, and add an upgrade slot to each. All of these such tools are priced at GM discretion.

  • Result 7 - Materials to turn 2 regular quality ranged weapons into excellent quality ranged weapons, and add an upgrade slot to each.

  • Result 8 - Materials to add an option slot to 2 pieces of cyberware, or 1 piece of borgware.

  • Result 9 - A collection of blackmail materials among prominent gangs worth 7 downtime days

  • Result 10 - A prototype RatSec Mini Air Drone with dartgun; all its base stats are increased by 1, its armor is increased by 2, and it can be controlled via either an included remote as an action, or instantaneously via an included chip which can be inserted into a chipslot.

Your Feedback Wanted!

Did you enjoy this session? Did something go wrong? Was there broken content? I would very much like to hear about your successes and failures both.

If you have a few minutes after your session, please take the time to fill out the feedback survey for this campaign, located here. It should only take 5 minutes, and will be actively considered as I make revisions to the campaign.

Your feedback matters! Thank you for playing!

The Vampyres are unquestionably one of the most complex sets of mooks in NCG&C, and certainly the most elaborate. They can be overwhelming at first, so let's take this one step at a time and make them easier.

Tip 1: Give Yourself Setup Time

Roll your set dressing as soon as you know you're going to be using the Vamps, and familiarize yourself with the abilities it provides immediately. Keep the entry open while you're running the scene, and assign simple, high-level roles to the Vamps as they're outlined here.

Tip 2: Pick a Theme, Then Stick To It

Ask yourself: "what's my theme?" The Vamps are an attempt to let you do the incredible, over-the-top set pieces from movies like Pirates of the Caribbean or Mad Max. Or introduce a circus where a gang throwdown should be. Or recreate that one scene with the animals from RRR (seriously - look up RRR Tiger Fight Scene on Youtube, you won't regret it).

Figure out what your Vamps will be wearing, and what their jobs will be during the beat.

Tip 3: Keep It Light, Keep It Nonlethal

The Vampyres are hated by corps and gangs, and beloved by the people of Night City, for being the very essence of the flamboyant mirror to the setting's power abuses and cyberpsychos. And that's not an accident - the Vamps deliberately cultivate that mystique and persona as a way to keep making headlines. They punish and humiliate those who deserve it, give freely to the poor and helpless, and laugh easily no matter how dire the circumstances. In a world of nihilistic decay, they've chosen to pursue absurdism, instead.

In short: in the cyberpunk dystopia where corps, gangs and governments grind the human spirit to dust, the Vampyres use their talents to fight back.

In that vein, they load their guns with rubber bullets, and their pyrotechnics might injure someone, but they're mostly concussive - nonlethal, so far as they can help it. They get away with it through raw skill and talent, alongside well coordinated choreography, making fools of those they choose to fight.

Tip 4: Play Around The Master Of Ceremonies

The centerpiece of any Vampyre show will be the MC. They're the most elaborately dressed, the main character, the master of the spotlight. And that takes a lot of work out of figuring out what you're doing.

Think of it like a stageplay or dance routine - the protagonist is the focus, and the others are there to accentuate whatever they're doing. If the MC jumps, the performers jump too.

Tip 5: Know Your Roles

The MCs are few and far between, and the focal point of the gang's activity. They dictate what's going on and how it's happening.

The Showstoppers accentuate the MC's actions with their extra array of special effects - an explosion to go with a rock opera's crescendo. Leaping tigers and wolves to accompany an acrobatic dance through the air.

The Performers follow the MC's actions, providing extra sets of hands to give those actions oomph.

Tip 6: Coordinate your efforts, accomplish a goal

Every set (save Caution: Live Animals) has one thing in common, and that's their 3 point crescendo. Every show is centered around that particular beat, and structured to take advantage of whatever it provides. So do so!

Tip 7: Experiment, Try, Try Again

You won't get it perfect the first time, but even with all the flaws and mistakes you might make, it'll still probably be one of the most ridiculous, over-the-top, memorable sessions your players will ever have.

Don't worry yourself about getting it perfectly right. Just. Have. Fun.

pages.name: Testing Session - How I Planned My Vampyres

text.content:
Below are my actual notes for how I prepped and ran this scene, edited for readability.

So, Some Theater Kids Decide To Rob A Bank…

My initial set dressing was acrobatics and agility.

My showstopper set dressing was over the top effects

Step 1: Set Up The Show

The vampyres infiltrate the crowd; their show begins the moment the first player enters the bank. The MC begins speaking to attract security, jumping up onto the offices in the lobby to attract attention; their crew hoist the execs into the air using the 3 point ability, but leave the security alone (they want to do a show, after all). The 3 point ability isn't charged since it's not being used on the players. These execs will be "held hostage" by the crew, stopping the NCPD from immediately breaching the building.

Civillians will be asked to gather in the lobby in front of the teller area.

While security scrambles, a pair of performers will hoist a ratsec grunt using their 2 point ability + assistance to set the DV to beat for the round. The others will begin dispersing around the lobby, using their action to climb and unlock the ability to dodge bullets.

Harrying The Party

The two showstoppers will use their luck entirely to hamper the party as they approach, moving automatically towards wherever the party enters the scene. They're already mounted (rolling desks or mop buckets, maybe) when they reach the players. Their goal is to impose penalties on the party to stop them from stealing the spotlight.

Step 2: Robbing The Petty Cash Vault

Once they've gotten everyone's attention, the MC will head to the petty cash vault in the lobby and open it using their technical skill. They and their crew will alternate between dealing with security and taking actions on the vault, tossing the loot out into the crowd.

Once the vault's looted or inaccessible, the MC will continue robbing the main vault until the NCPD breach in beat 4.

Step 3: Escape

Once the NCPD breaches, the Vampyres will attempt to escape by climbing to rooftops using their rope systems and acrobatics. If they can't escape, they'll surrender peacefully.
